category_title: Preschoolers with aggressive behaviors and social-emotional regulation difficulties
minimal_context: Joshua is playing with toy trucks in the preschool classroom during free play and is asked to share materials with a classmate.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Joshua, it is free play time, so please share the toy trucks and build a road with your classmate.

STUDENT: (Holds the toy trucks tightly) No, I am the boss of the trucks. I am not sharing.

TUTOR: You need to take turns with the toys.

STUDENT: (Growls) Grrr! I make the rules! He has to be the worker who just watches!

TUTOR: He wants to drive a truck too. How can we play together?

STUDENT: (Yells and throws a toy) No! Do this or I will hurt you!

options.A: Utilize regulated settings that allow Joshua to acquire sharing skills with the toy trucks, and then transition him to the classroom setting with peers.
options.B: Read the social stories (It is Hard to Share, I Get Angry Sometimes, How we Play Together) to the whole group every day before free-play time, ensuring Joshua has time to process them.
options.C: Gradually fade the amount of scaffolding provided during free-play time as Joshua's competence with sharing the toy trucks and playing with peers increases.
options.D: Model complete sentences as often as possible during free-play time when Joshua is playing with the toy trucks and interacting with his peers.
